backdrops.note: This act introduces the main characters, world, and central premise. It establishes the status quo and presents the inciting incident that disrupts it. By the end, the protagonist commits to a course of action, setting the main story in motion.
backdrops.title: Act One : Setup

backdrops.note: The protagonist begins pursuing their goal but encounters early obstacles and complications. Stakes start to build, and relationships and conflicts deepen. This act often ends with a turning point that raises the tension significantly.
backdrops.title: Act Two : Rising Action

backdrops.note: The story reaches a major turning point or revelation that changes the direction of the narrative. This could be a victory, defeat, or major discovery, but it reframes the protagonist’s understanding of the conflict and raises the stakes further.
backdrops.title: Act Three : Climax (Midpoint)

backdrops.note: Consequences of the midpoint unfold, and things begin to spiral or intensify toward a breaking point. The protagonist faces their greatest challenges, often leading to a low moment where success seems out of reach.
backdrops.title: Act Four : Complications

backdrops.note: The protagonist confronts the central conflict in the climax and resolves it. The story then winds down, showing the aftermath and how the characters or world have changed.
backdrops.title: Act Five : Resolution
